sections.title: Introduction to Articles
sections.content: Articles are words that precede nouns to indicate their gender and number. French has definite articles (the), indefinite articles (a/an), and partitive articles (some/any).

sections.title: Definite Articles
sections.content: Definite articles (le, la, l', les) refer to specific nouns that have already been mentioned or are known to the speaker and listener. Le for masculine singular, la for feminine singular, l' for singular nouns starting with a vowel, les for plural.
examples: l'arbre (the tree) - masculine singular starting with vowel
l'école (the school) - feminine singular starting with vowel

sections.title: Indefinite Articles
sections.content: Indefinite articles (un, une, des) refer to non-specific nouns. Un for masculine singular, une for feminine singular, des for plural.

sections.title: Partitive Articles
sections.content: Partitive articles (du, de la, de l', des) are used with uncountable nouns to express an unspecified quantity. They are often translated as 'some' or 'any' in English.
examples: de l'eau (some water) - starts with vowel

sections.title: Negation with Articles
sections.content: With negation, definite and indefinite articles change to de or d'. The partitive article also becomes de or d'.
examples: Je vois le chat → Je ne vois pas le chat (I see the cat → I don't see the cat) - definite stays
J'ai un livre → Je n'ai pas de livre (I have a book → I don't have a book) - un/une becomes de
Je veux du pain → Je ne veux pas de pain (I want some bread → I don't want any bread) - partitive becomes de

sections.title: Contractions with À and De
sections.content: Definite articles contract with à and de: à + le = au, à + les = aux, de + le = du, de + les = des.
examples: Je vais au marché (I go to the market) - à + le
Je parle aux enfants (I speak to the children) - à + les
Je reviens du cinéma (I come back from the cinema) - de + le
Je reviens des États-Unis (I come back from the United States) - de + les

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Write 20 sentences using different articles. Include examples of definite, indefinite, partitive articles, and their use in negation and contractions.
